commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement Extended Link Opaque LSA parser and database detail display

- Parse Extended Link TLV (RFC 7684 §3) with Adj-SID (RFC 8665 §5)
  and LAN Adj-SID (RFC 8665 §6) Sub-TLVs
- Add show database detail output for Extended Link LSAs
- Clean up remaining debug println statements in ifsm.rs and inst.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement OSPF SR TLV parsing and database detail display

- Move SidLabelTlv to packet-utils crate for sharing between isis-packet and ospf-packet
- Add OSPF-style SID/Label Sub-TLV parser (2+2 byte header vs IS-IS 1+1)
- Parse RouterInfo TLVs: SID/Label Range, Local Block, Router Capability, Algorithm
- Implement Extended Prefix Opaque LSA parser (RFC 7684) with Prefix SID Sub-TLV (RFC 8665)
- Add "show ip ospf database detail" output for Opaque Area LSAs with SR details
- Clean up debug println statements in nfsm.rs

Build new file creation flow, fix date timezone bug, improve UX (#14)

- Add NewFileModal component for creating markdown files in collections
  with modal form collecting title, auto-generating YYYY-MM-DD-{slug}.md
- Create POST /api/github/[owner]/[repo]/new-file API route using GitHub
  App installation token, pre-populating frontmatter (title, date, author,
  tags, published, description)
- Enable "+ New file" button in FileListPanel, wire to modal
- Fix frontmatter panel showing empty fields on newly created files by using
  `sha` instead of `bodyHtml` as readiness signal (empty body was falsy,
  blocking initialization)
- Add loading skeleton to frontmatter panel (4 animated placeholder bars)
- Fix date display bug where YYYY-MM-DD dates showed one day behind in
  UTC- timezones: use getUTC* methods instead of local getters, handle
  gray-matter Date objects in asString()
- Add semantic color aliases to Tailwind @theme (--color-surface,
  --color-fg, --color-border) that components use as bg-surface, text-fg,
  etc. but were missing from theme definition, causing transparent backgrounds
- Add "view on GitHub" link (ExternalLink icon) next to editor file path in
  topbar, styled as subtle icon-only button

commit.message:
Add NPC rival corps, PvP combat, and arbitrage UI

NPC corps (Helios Mining, Apex Industries, Ironclad Freight) are Player rows
with is_npc=True. Their ships are regular Ship rows processed by the existing
mission tick — no separate infrastructure. Combat with them uses the same
/game/attack endpoint as PvP. Server seeds corps on startup (idempotent) and
dispatches idle ships to random asteroids every game-hour.

Also adds arbitrage section to market tab showing best sell prices across all
10 trading hubs, and full PvP combat system (proximity check, weapon resolution,
SSE notification to defender).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add MatmulOperationDescriptor to backend C-API and frontend lowering (#5154)

## Summary

Implements the matmul operation descriptor for the hipDNN backend C-API
and wires up frontend graph lowering via the descriptor path, completing
the C-API surface for matmul operations.

- Adds `MatmulOperationDescriptor` backend descriptor with
`setAttribute`/`getAttribute` for A, B, C tensors and compute data type
- Registers `HIPDNN_BACKEND_OPERATION_MATMUL_DESCRIPTOR` in the
descriptor factory and enum string utilities
- Adds `MatmulPacker` frontend helper and `create_operation()` override
on `MatmulNode` for C-API lowering
- 35 backend unit tests covering lifecycle, attribute setting/getting,
finalization validation, `buildNode`, and `IGraphOperation` interface
- 2 frontend integration tests verifying round-trip serialization
through the descriptor lowering path

Resolves: ALMIOPEN-1121

## Files Changed

**Backend (new descriptor):**
- `HipdnnBackendDescriptorType.h` — new
`HIPDNN_BACKEND_OPERATION_MATMUL_DESCRIPTOR` enum
- `HipdnnBackendAttributeName.h` — new matmul attributes (1800–1803)
- `MatmulOperationDescriptor.hpp/.cpp` — descriptor implementation using
existing `DescriptorAttributeUtils`
- `DescriptorFactory.cpp` — factory registration
- `BackendEnumStringUtils.hpp` — string conversions

**Frontend (lowering):**
- `MatmulPacker.hpp` — `createMatmulOperation()` inline helper
- `MatmulNode.hpp` — `create_operation()` override

**Tests:**
- `TestMatmulOperationDescriptor.cpp` — 35 backend unit tests
- `IntegrationMatmulDescriptorLowering.cpp` — 2 frontend integration
round-trip tests

## Test plan

- [x] All 691 backend unit tests pass (`hipdnn_backend_tests`)
- [x] All 82 frontend tests pass including 2 new matmul lowering
integration tests (`public_hipdnn_frontend_tests`)
- [x] Run provider tests with feature flag
- [ ] CI pipeline passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

fix: DOCX equations, %pip cell execution, and runtime cache UX

  switch KaTeX to htmlAndMathml output so annotation elements are available
- Fix %pip install: run pip installs separately via pyodide, then execute
  remaining cell code through executePythonLegacy with eager matplotlib
  hook setup so plt.show() works on first run
- Preserve CDN cache on Reload App: only clear app caches, not Pyodide/
  swipl-wasm CDN cache
- Skip download prompt for cached runtimes: check actual CDN cache entries
  to determine if runtime was previously downloaded
- Add per-runtime Clear Cache button in Memory & Storage: allows removing
  cached CDN files for a specific runtime without affecting others
- Distinguish "Loading" (cached) vs "Downloading" (CDN) in progress text

feat: full H1-H7 hypothesis testing in Consumatori 3000 Interpretare

Add complete OSF hypothesis testing section with 7 collapsible OsfH blocks
to Consumatori 3000 Interpretare, mirroring OSF Statistic tab structure:
- H1: Multiplicativ vs Aditiv (Spearman, Fisher Z, Partial r)
- H2: Poarta Relevantei with gate stats, Cohen's d for Cp and CTA
- H3: Brand Moderator C→CTA (split by brand, Fisher Z comparison)
- H4: C→CTA correlation (Pearson r, regression r²)
- H5: Cross-Channel Cronbach α table with inter-item correlations
- H6: Segment Sensitivity ANOVA (η² per demographic factor)
- H7: Construct Validity r(Cf, Cp) with validation % and delta
- MODELUL RIFC info box with formula explanation
- Summary table with all 7 hypotheses, metrics, and verdicts
All computed from Consumatori-filtered data (calibrated respondents ≥70%).

commit.message:
security: comprehensive hardening — OWASP Top 10 audit fixes

- Remove all hardcoded secrets (OpenRouter key, Supabase key, proxy creds, Airtable ID)
- Add Helmet.js security headers to proxy server
- Add express-rate-limit (300/15min general, 100/15min scrape)
- Add API token auth middleware (PROXY_API_TOKEN) on all 19 endpoints
- Restrict CORS to configured origins only (CORS_ORIGINS env var)
- Add input validation (username/subreddit regex) on all scrape routes
- Add SSRF protection on AI base URL (allowlisted hosts only)
- Fix XSS in main.jsx (innerHTML -> textContent via DOM API)
- Harden PIN auth: remove default 1234, add rate limiting (5/60s)
- Add getProxyHeaders() for x-api-token injection on all proxy calls
- Add proxyApiToken to settings + textKeys
- Fix .gitignore to catch all .env files
- Add .env.example template
- Add supabase_rls_policies.sql for RLS enablement
- Reduce verbose profile logging (no PII)
- Reduce payload limit 5MB -> 1MB

fix: skip create_all() in production to avoid conflict with Alembic

PostgreSQL schema is managed by Alembic migrations; calling
Base.metadata.create_all() in production causes duplicate key errors
on enum types and tables already created by migrations.

terminal so the `read` confirmation prompt blocks forever. Now auto-
confirms when stdin is not a TTY or when --yes/-y flag is passed.

commit.message:
Classify ErrorDuplicateVirtual → §8.18 [skip ci]

§8.18 "Data hiding and encapsulation" states that modifiers such as virtual can only appear once per member and that it shall be an error to duplicate any of the other modifiers. The test exercises exactly this rule by using `virtual virtual` on a method declaration.

fix: default ffmpeg timeout flag to -timeout for Trixie/ffmpeg 7.x compatibility

On Debian Trixie (ffmpeg 7.x), -stimeout was removed. The fallback
default now uses -timeout which works on ffmpeg 5.x+ and is required
for ffmpeg 7.x. Bumps version to 3.0.7.

commit.message:
Add 12 new capabilities: 7 Siri intents + 5 chat tools, Build 128

New Siri Intents (7):
- WithdrawNagIntent — cancel a nag you sent
- DismissNagIntent — hide a received nag
- CommitTimeIntent — commit to when you'll do a nag
- PressNagIntent — escalate/update nag message
- SubmitExcuseIntent — excuse for overdue nag
- InviteConnectionIntent — invite by email
- DisconnectIntent — remove a peer connection

New Chat Tools (5):
- withdrawNag — cancel nags via chat
- dismissNag — hide nags via chat
- commitTime — commit to time via chat
- disconnectConnection — remove connections via chat
- familyStatus — weekly family report via chat

10 shortcuts registered in NagzShortcutsProvider (Apple max).
All 14 intents available as regular Shortcuts actions.
Chat and Siri now have full feature parity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add step-interval checkpointing and auto-resume to drafter training

Saves optimizer/scheduler/model state every --save_steps (default 5000).
Auto-resumes from drafter_latest.pt on restart — critical for preemptible VMs
where epochs are 85K+ steps apart.

commit.message:
feat(renderer): data bridge — props injection via dataKey [step-4]

Thread data?: Record<string, unknown> through the full pipeline:
RenderedPage → render() → runPipeline() → stepNormalize() → PipelineContext.

In step2-resolve-components: blocks with props.dataKey receive
{ data: ctx.data[dataKey], dataKey } merged into node.props at render time.

Usage: <RenderedPage wireframe={doc} data={{ events: apiData }} />

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Claude/rebase and documentation (#5)

* Add feature recommendations for BinManager

Comprehensive analysis of gaps and improvement opportunities
across 15 recommended features, organized by priority:
- High: import/restore, item editing, bin editing, photo compression
- Medium: sorting, tags, custom dialogs, archiving, pagination
- Lower: sync, barcodes, stats, bulk ops, a11y, print contents

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UMMMPtyc5KEEJyFdeirAzY

* Implement 10 features: import, edit, compress, tags, archive, sort, paginate, print

Features implemented:
1. Data Import/Restore - JSON file import with merge/replace modes and validation
2. Edit Items - Reuses item form with pre-filled values, preserves addedAt
3. Edit Bins - Reuses bin form with pre-filled values, preserves createdAt
4. Photo Compression - Canvas API resize (800px max, JPEG 0.7 quality)
5. Item Sorting/Filtering - Sort dropdown (newest/oldest/a-z/z-a), persisted in localStorage
6. Item Tags - Comma-separated input, deduplicated, displayed as chips, Fuse searchable
7. Custom Confirmation Dialogs - Promise-based modal replacing all confirm() calls
8. Bin Archive/Soft Delete - Toggle archive with badge, filtered from search by default
9. Pagination/Index Caching - dataVersion counter for Fuse cache, 20 items/page with Load More
15. Print Bin Contents - Print button on bin detail with @media print CSS

Architecture decisions validated by expert review:
- No IndexedDB version bump needed (tags via Fuse.js, archive filtered in JS)
- Export nav replaced with Data Management view (export + import)
- Import validates structure (bins/items arrays, required id/binId fields)
- Export includes schema version field for future compatibility
- Tags normalized to lowercase with deduplication

Also includes comprehensive browser-based test suite (tests/test.html).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UMMMPtyc5KEEJyFdeirAzY

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

GPS display: init CAN + send heartbeats before GPS fix

CAN bus and listener were only initialized after acquiring a satellite
fix, so the module was invisible on the bus while waiting indoors.
Move CAN init before WaitForGPSD and send heartbeats during the wait
loop so the GPS module is a good CAN citizen from startup.
